title: How Algeria tried to erase the two suicide attacks during Pope Leo's visit
subhead: Two men blew themselves up on Monday about 40 kilometers from Algiers, without causing any casualties other than themselves. Authorities sought to impose silence on the failed terrorist attack from the official narrative of the historic visit.

title: Why the Hormuz 'mission' led by France and the UK is largely symbolic

French businesses will (mostly) remain closed on Labor Day as government backs down
subhead: Despite efforts by MPs from President Emmanuel Macron's party to allow local businesses such as bakeries and flower shops to open on May 1, Labor Day will remain a mandatory paid holiday in 2026.

title: French MPs vote to scrap low-emission zones
subhead: The Assemblée Nationale on Tuesday chose to repeal a proposal to implement low-emission zones, the Sénat is likely to do the same on Wednesday. Socialist, Green and centrist MPs said they would refer the matter to the Constitutional Council.

title: Calder at the Fondation Louis Vuitton in Paris, a festive experience
subhead: The exhibition spans the sculptor's entire body of work and takes over the entire building, spilling out onto the exterior. The journey is a joyful celebration, filled with lightness and movement.

title: Firing of respected French publisher reveals billionaire Bolloré's tightening grip
subhead: Olivier Nora, the long-time CEO of the Grasset publishing house, was fired on Tuesday. He was replaced with a close associate of Vincent Bolloré, who took control of France's leading publishing group Hachette Livre in 2023.

Hungary enters new era following Orban's defeat
subhead: During a three-hour press conference, Péter Magyar, the conservative yet pro-European winner of the election, maintained a degree of ambiguity on the subject of Ukraine but outlined a return to the rule of law for the small Central European country.

title: Jordan Bardella, a princess and 'Paris Match': The story of a PR gamble
subhead: The relationship between Marine Le Pen's lieutenant and an aristocratic student raised in the international jet set made the front page of 'Paris Match.' The media empire of Vincent Bolloré has seized the opportunity to 'glamorize' the likely far-right presidential candidate.

title: One year ahead of French presidential election, survey shows deep desire for change
subhead: Following the municipal elections, the political landscape is marked by a favorable perception of the far-right Rassemblement National and by highly polarized preferences among French voters, a Le Monde survey shows.
